**Default:** When `formatValue` is not provided, the tooltip uses a formatter that reads the data point’s `y` property (same as the chart's default Y accessor), converts it to a string, and returns an empty string for null. This is correct for the default `DataPoint` shape (`{ x, y }`).

**Custom:** Pass `formatValue` to control display. The function receives the datum and returns a string. Use it to both extract and format (e.g. currency, percentage, custom units). For custom data point shapes (generic `TooltipProps<YourDatum>`), you must pass `formatValue` because the default only reads `y`.

```tsx
<Tooltip
  tooltipData={tooltipData}
  series={series}
  formatValue={(d) => `$${d.y?.toLocaleString() ?? ''}`}
/>
```

Examples [#examples]

Single Series [#single-series]

Basic tooltip with a single series and default formatting.

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceSingleSeries" />

<Story of="Stories.SingleSeries" />

Multiple Series [#multiple-series]

Tooltip with multiple series; all series at the same X position are shown together.

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceMultipleSeries" />

<Story of="Stories.MultipleSeries" />

Arrow [#arrow]

Use the `showArrow` prop to set whether the tooltip arrow is visible or not.

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceArrow" />

<Story of="Stories.Arrow" />


# LineChart





<Meta of="Stories" name="Overview" />

Installation [#installation]

```bash
npm install --save @godaddy/antares
```

Props [#props]

<PropTable />

Examples [#examples]

Basic [#basic]

Single Series [#single-series]

One line with time-scale X-axis and minimal configuration.

<Story of="Stories.SingleSeries" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceSingleSeries" />

Multiple Series [#multiple-series]

Several lines with a bottom legend and shared X-axis.

<Story of="Stories.MultipleSeries" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceMultipleSeries" />

Axis [#axis]

Gridlines [#gridlines]

Vertical and horizontal gridlines via `xGridlines` and `yGridlines`.

<Story of="Stories.Gridlines" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceGridlines" />

Titles [#titles]

X- and Y-axis titles to label what each axis represents (e.g. "Month", "Temperature (°F)").

<Story of="Stories.Titles" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceTitles" />

Labels [#labels]

Tick labels on both axes with `xLabels` and `yLabels`.

<Story of="Stories.Labels" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceLabels" />

Ticks [#ticks]

Tick marks (short lines) at each tick value. Controlled by `xTickMarks` and `yTickMarks`.

<Story of="Stories.Ticks" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceTicks" />

Baselines [#baselines]

Axis baseline (the main axis line). Controlled by `xBaseline` and `yBaseline`.

<Story of="Stories.Baselines" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceBaselines" />

Legend [#legend]

Legend showing series names and colors, positioned above or below the chart via `legendPosition`.

<Story of="Stories.Legend" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceLegend" />

Formatting [#formatting]

Axis and Tooltip Formatting [#axis-and-tooltip-formatting]

Custom formatters for axis ticks and tooltip values (e.g. currency, dates). Controlled by `xTickFormat` and `yTickFormat`.

<Story of="Stories.Formatting" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceFormatting" />

Scale [#scale]

Fixed Domain [#fixed-domain]

Explicit X and Y domain for a fixed range or consistent scale across charts.

<Story of="Stories.FixedDomain" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceFixedDomain" />

Nice Values [#nice-values]

Domain extended to nice round values (e.g. 0.2–1.0).

<Story of="Stories.NiceValues" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceNiceValues" />

Zero Included [#zero-included]

X and Y domains include zero via `xZero` and `yZero`.

<Story of="Stories.ZeroIncluded" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceZeroIncluded" />

Custom Ticks [#custom-ticks]

Explicit tick values or count with `xTickValues` / `yTickValues` or `xNumTicks` / `yNumTicks`.

<Story of="Stories.CustomTicks" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceCustomTicks" />

Band Padding [#band-padding]

Outer padding on a band-scale axis with `xPaddingOuter` / `yPaddingOuter`.

<Story of="Stories.BandPadding" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceBandPadding" />

Interactive [#interactive]

Tooltip Disabled [#tooltip-disabled]

Tooltip, crosshair, and data point glyphs all disabled (`tooltip={false}`, `showCrosshair={false}`, `showDataPoints={false}`).

<Story of="Stories.TooltipDisabled" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceTooltipDisabled" />

Crosshair Only [#crosshair-only]

Only the vertical crosshair on hover; tooltip and data point glyphs disabled.

<Story of="Stories.CrosshairOnly" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceCrosshairOnly" />

Custom Tooltip Formatting [#custom-tooltip-formatting]

Tooltip values formatted with `tooltipValueFormatter` (e.g. currency, units).

<Story of="Stories.CustomTooltipFormatting" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceCustomTooltipFormatting" />

Data [#data]

Custom Accessors [#custom-accessors]

Custom data shape (e.g. `date`/`value`) with `xAccessor` and `yAccessor`.

<Story of="Stories.CustomAccessors" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceCustomAccessors" />

Missing Values [#missing-values]

Null Y values create breaks in the line.

<Story of="Stories.MissingValues" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceMissingValues" />

Layout [#layout]

Fixed Size [#fixed-size]

Explicit chart size via `width` and `height` props (e.g. 800×400).

<Story of="Stories.FixedSize" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceFixedSize" />

Featured [#featured]

Bitcoin Price [#bitcoin-price]

Time-series with date and currency formatting, gridlines, and nice Y-axis.

<Story of="Stories.BitcoinPrice" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceBitcoinPrice" />

City Temperature [#city-temperature]

Multiple series over time with legend, shared time axis, and temperature (°F) formatting.

<Story of="Stories.CityTemperature" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceCityTemperature" />

Browser Usage [#browser-usage]

Categorical X-axis (band scale) with multiple series and percentage formatting.

<Story of="Stories.BrowserUsage" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceBrowserUsage" />

Customization [#customization]

Data Attributes [#data-attributes]

The chart root exposes data attributes that reflect prop state for styling:

**Chart container:** `data-width`, `data-height`, `data-x-labels`, `data-y-labels`, `data-x-labels-vertical`, `data-x-baseline`, `data-y-baseline`, `data-x-tick-marks`, `data-y-tick-marks`

Component Customization [#component-customization]

Use the `className` prop on the chart container to add or override styles:

```tsx
<LineChart series={series} className="my-custom-chart" />
```

Accessibility [#accessibility]

ARIA Support [#aria-support]

* Provide &#x2A;*`aria-label`** to describe the chart (e.g. "Monthly sales revenue from January to May 2024").
* Use &#x2A;*`desc`** for a longer description rendered in the SVG `<desc>` for screen readers.
* The component sets ARIA attributes on interactive and structural elements as needed.

Troubleshooting [#troubleshooting]

Chart Not Sizing or Empty [#chart-not-sizing-or-empty]

Ensure the chart has a non-zero size. Place it in a container with explicit dimensions or use `width` and `height`:

```tsx
// ✅ Container with size
<div style={{ width: '100%', height: 400 }}>
  <LineChart series={series} />
</div>

// ✅ Or fixed dimensions on the component
<LineChart series={series} width={600} height={400} />
```

Dates Not Displaying Correctly [#dates-not-displaying-correctly]

Use `xType="time"` when the X-axis values are Date objects:

```tsx
// ❌ Without xType="time", Date objects may not scale correctly
<LineChart series={seriesWithDates} />

// ✅ Use time scale for Date X values
<LineChart series={seriesWithDates} xType="time" />
```

Tooltip or Crosshair Not Showing [#tooltip-or-crosshair-not-showing]

Ensure `tooltip`, `showCrosshair`, or `showDataPoints` are not set to `false`, and that the chart receives pointer events (e.g. no overlay blocking hover).

Best Practices [#best-practices]

When to Use LineChart [#when-to-use-linechart]

* Use for trends over time or along a continuous scale.
* Use when comparing a few series (e.g. 2–5 lines); avoid too many overlapping lines.
* Prefer a single chart with a legend over multiple small charts when comparing the same X range.


# TooltipDismissStrip







<Meta of="Stories" name="Overview" />

Internal Component [#internal-component]

This component is **internal-only** and not exported from the package. It is designed for use within chart components.

Props [#props]

The `TooltipDismissStrip` component accepts the following props:

<PropTable />

Examples [#examples]

Basic [#basic]

Tooltip provider, relative container, optional controls to open the visx tooltip and the dismiss strip on the leading edge.

<Story of="Stories.Basic" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceBasic" />


# useChartColor







<Meta of="Stories" name="Overview" />

For use with chart components (not exported) [#for-use-with-chart-components-not-exported]

Wrap your chart (or list) with `ChartColorProvider`; each component that calls `useChartColor()` receives a CSS variable reference. Indices are assigned in an effect at mount, so allocation is safe under React concurrent rendering and StrictMode. Same component instance keeps the same color; colors cycle after the ninth consumer. No need to pass an index—order in the tree determines assignment.

Props [#props]

<PropTable />

Examples [#examples]

Single series [#single-series]

One series using the first chart color.

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceSingleSeries" />

<Story of="Stories.SingleSeries" />

Multiple series [#multiple-series]

Multiple series each receiving the next color in sequence.

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceMultiSeries" />

<Story of="Stories.MultiSeries" />


# useNormalizedSeries



For use with chart components (not exported) [#for-use-with-chart-components-not-exported]

Accepts a `SeriesConfig[]` array and returns a memoized copy where each item is guaranteed to have an `id`. When an item has no `id`, one is generated using a component-scoped prefix from React's `useId`, ensuring IDs are unique across multiple chart instances on the same page. Existing `id` values are preserved as-is.

```tsx
const series = useNormalizedSeries(seriesProp);
```


# Box







<Meta of="Stories" name="Overview" />

Box is a polymorphic layout component that renders as any HTML element or custom component via the `as` prop. It provides consistent spacing, elevation, rounding, and self-alignment within flex or grid containers.

**Important**: Since `Flex` and `Grid` extend `Box`, prefer them for layouts. Use `Box` only when you need a non-flex, non-grid container.

Installation [#installation]

```bash
npm install --save @godaddy/antares
```

Props [#props]

The `Box` component accepts the following props:

<PropTable />

Scale Sizes [#scale-sizes]

The spacing props use semantic t-shirt sizes for padding, gap, and other spacing properties:

* `xs` - Extra small spacing
* `sm` - Small spacing
* `md` - Medium spacing
* `lg` - Large spacing
* `xl` - Extra large spacing
* `2xl` - 2x extra large spacing

Custom CSS values are also supported for edge cases.

Examples [#examples]

Padding [#padding]

The padding props support t-shirt sizes and CSS logical properties for RTL support.

<Story of="Stories.Padding" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourcePadding" />

Elevation [#elevation]

The elevation prop adds visual depth using box-shadow.

<Story of="Stories.Elevation" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceElevation" />

Rounding [#rounding]

The rounding prop sets border-radius. Use `full` for fully rounded corners.

<Story of="Stories.Rounding" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceRounding" />

Polymorphic `as` Prop [#polymorphic-as-prop]

Box is fully polymorphic - the `as` prop allows rendering as any HTML element or custom component while preserving type-safe props.

**HTML Elements:** When using `as="button"`, TypeScript enforces button-specific props like `type`, `disabled`, and `onClick`.

**Custom Components:** When using `as={MyComponent}`, Box accepts that component's props. If there's a prop name conflict, Box's props take priority.

**Typed Refs:** Refs are correctly typed based on the `as` value - `useRef<HTMLButtonElement>` works with `as="button"`.

<Story of="Stories.As" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceAs" />

Alignment [#alignment]

Self-alignment props allow Box to position itself within flex or grid containers.

<Story of="Stories.Alignment" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceAlignment" />

RTL Support [#rtl-support]

Box uses CSS logical properties (`padding-inline-start`, `padding-block-end`, etc.) which automatically adapt to RTL layouts. No additional configuration is needed.

Design Tokens [#design-tokens]

All spacing, elevation, and rounding values are derived from the theme. If no theme is provided, default values are used. This ensures consistency across the design system.


# Flex







<Meta of="Stories" name="Overview" />

Flex is a one-dimensional layout component for arranging children in rows or columns with alignment and spacing control. It extends [`Box`](../box/README.mdx), inheriting all Box capabilities (padding, elevation, rounding, self-alignment, and polymorphic `as` prop).

**Recommended default**: Use Flex for most layouts. Since it extends Box, you get all Box capabilities without needing to nest components.

Installation [#installation]

```bash
npm install --save @godaddy/antares
```

Props [#props]

The `Flex` component accepts the following props:

<PropTable />

Scale Sizes [#scale-sizes]

We use the same scale sizes as the `Box` component. For more information, see the [**Box component documentation**](../box/README.mdx#scale-sizes).

Examples [#examples]

Default [#default]

Basic horizontal flex layout with gap.

<Story of="Stories.Default" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceDefault" />

Direction [#direction]

The `direction` prop controls the main axis orientation.

<Story of="Stories.Direction" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceDirection" />

Alignment [#alignment]

Use `justifyContent` for main-axis distribution and `alignItems` for cross-axis alignment.

<Story of="Stories.Alignment" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceAlignment" />

Gap [#gap]

The `gap` prop controls spacing between children. Use `rowGap` and `columnGap` for independent control.

<Story of="Stories.Gap" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceGap" />

Wrap [#wrap]

The `wrap` prop controls whether items wrap to multiple lines.

<Story of="Stories.Wrap" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceWrap" />

Navbar [#navbar]

A common navigation bar pattern using space-between and nested Flex containers.

<Story of="Stories.Navbar" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceNavbar" />

Sidebar [#sidebar]

A sidebar layout demonstrating Flex child props (`flexShrink`, `flexGrow`).

<Story of="Stories.Sidebar" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceSidebar" />

RTL Support [#rtl-support]

Flex uses CSS logical properties which automatically adapt to RTL layouts. The `direction` prop values (`row`, `row-reverse`) respect the document direction.

Design Tokens [#design-tokens]

All gap and padding values are derived from the theme. If no theme is provided, default values are used. This ensures consistency across the design system.

Migration from @ux/box [#migration-from-uxbox]

| Current `@ux/box`                    | New Pattern                      |
| ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------- |
| `<Box orientation="horizontal">`     | `<Flex direction="row" >`        |
| `<Box orientation="vertical">`       | `<Flex direction="column" >`     |
| `<Box blockAlignChildren="center">`  | `<Flex justifyContent="center">` |
| `<Box inlineAlignChildren="center">` | `<Flex alignItems="center">`     |


# Grid







<Meta of="Stories" name="Overview" />

Grid is a two-dimensional CSS Grid layout component for complex grid-based layouts. It extends [`Box`](../box/README.mdx), inheriting all Box capabilities (padding, elevation, rounding, self-alignment, and polymorphic `as` prop).

**Use Grid when**: You need explicit two-dimensional layouts with rows and columns, named grid areas, or responsive auto-fill patterns.

Installation [#installation]

```bash
npm install --save @godaddy/antares
```

Props [#props]

The `Grid` component accepts the following props:

<PropTable />

Scale Sizes [#scale-sizes]

We use the same scale sizes as the `Box` component. For more information, see the [**Box component documentation**](../box/README.mdx#scale-sizes).

Examples [#examples]

Default [#default]

Basic 3-column grid layout with gap.

<Story of="Stories.Default" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceDefault" />

Columns [#columns]

The `columns` prop defines grid column tracks using CSS grid-template-columns syntax.

<Story of="Stories.Columns" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceColumns" />

Gap [#gap]

The `gap` prop controls spacing between grid cells. Use `rowGap` and `columnGap` for independent control.

<Story of="Stories.Gap" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceGap" />

Named Areas [#named-areas]

Use `areas` to define named grid regions. Children use `gridArea` prop for placement.

<Story of="Stories.Areas" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceAreas" />

Responsive Grid [#responsive-grid]

Use CSS `auto-fill` and `minmax()` for responsive column counts without media queries.

<Story of="Stories.Responsive" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceResponsive" />

Alignment [#alignment]

Use `justifyItems` and `alignItems` to control default alignment of all grid children.

<Story of="Stories.Alignment" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceAlignment" />

Form Layout [#form-layout]

A common two-column form pattern with auto-sized label column.

<Story of="Stories.Form" />

<Source language="tsx" code="SourceForm" />

RTL Support [#rtl-support]

Grid uses CSS logical properties which automatically adapt to RTL layouts. Grid areas and alignment respect the document direction.

Grid vs Flex [#grid-vs-flex]

| Use Case                   | Recommended      |
| -------------------------- | ---------------- |
| Single row/column of items | Flex             |
| Two-dimensional layouts    | Grid             |
| Named layout regions       | Grid             |
| Auto-wrapping cards        | Grid (auto-fill) |
| Navigation bars            | Flex             |
| Form layouts               | Grid             |
